conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me first review the official eligibility rules, then I'll begin asking you about your circumstances.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. I've reviewed the eligibility rules for Child Benefit. Now let me ask you some questions to assess your situation.

**First, do you have any children that you're asking about for Child Benefit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who's 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 18 years old, I need to ask about their education or training status, as this affects eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

(Approved education or training typically includes things like A-levels, T-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or certain vocational courses.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished school and aren't studying anymore.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Do you live with Alex, or are you contributing financially towards their upkeep (such as for food, clothes, or accommodation)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me also check one final detail.

**When did Alex finish their education or training - was it before or after they turned 20?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They finished school before turning 20 - they're only 18 now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the rules, Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for those under 20 who are in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and is no longer in approved education or training, you would not currently be eligible for Child Benefit for them.

However, it's worth noting that if Alex had left education or training within the last 20 weeks, you may have been able to claim Child Benefit during that period if they registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services.

Let me provide you with the formal assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry, but you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

**The reason:** While you are responsible for Alex (who lives with you in the UK), Child Benefit for children over 16 only continues if they remain in approved education or training until age 20. Since Alex is 18 and is no longer in any education or training, you do not qualify for Child Benefit.

If Alex decides to return to approved education or training in the future, you may be able to claim Child Benefit again at that point, as long as they are still under 20 years old.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and no longer in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training. Since Alex has finished school and is not currently in any approved education or training, the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for Alex. Child Benefit can only continue past age 16 if the young person stays in approved education or training (until they turn 20), or for up to 20 weeks after a 16 or 17 year old leaves education if they register with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. As Alex is 18 and not in education/training, none of these conditions are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old child Alex. While the claimant is responsible for Alex (who lives with them), Alex is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit eligibility for children over 16 requires that they remain in approved education or training until age 20. Since this condition is not met, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and no longer in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training. Since Alex has finished school and is not currently in any approved education or training, the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for Alex. Child Benefit can only continue past age 16 if the young person stays in approved education or training (until they turn 20), or for up to 20 weeks after a 16 or 17 year old leaves education if they register with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. As Alex is 18 and not in education/training, none of these conditions are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old child Alex. While the claimant is responsible for Alex (who lives with them), Alex is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit eligibility for children over 16 requires that they remain in approved education or training until age 20. Since this condition is not met, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
